safetyChecks.label: Measure blood pressure
safetyChecks.action: Document hypotension, severe hypertension, or arm-to-arm differences because these change acuity, monitoring, and vascular emergency concern.
safetyChecks.rationale: Chest pain: Blood pressure anchors hemodynamic safety and helps identify shock, hypertensive urgency, orthostasis, or medication-risk context before focused diagnosis-specific decisions.
safetyChecks.management_change: Document hypotension, severe hypertension, or arm-to-arm differences because these change acuity, monitoring, and vascular emergency concern.
safetyChecks.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
safetyChecks.diagnostic_target: hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context

safetyChecks.label: Measure heart rate
safetyChecks.action: Document tachycardia, bradycardia, or irregularity because these change ECG urgency, monitoring, and cardiopulmonary differential.
safetyChecks.rationale: Chest pain: Heart rate anchors physiologic severity and can reveal adrenergic state, hypovolemia, arrhythmia, infection severity, medication effect, or decompensation.
safetyChecks.management_change: Document tachycardia, bradycardia, or irregularity because these change ECG urgency, monitoring, and cardiopulmonary differential.
safetyChecks.limitations: Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
safetyChecks.diagnostic_target: tachycardia, bradycardia, irregular rhythm, or acute physiologic stress

safetyChecks.label: Measure respiratory rate
safetyChecks.action: Document tachypnea or respiratory distress because this changes PE, pneumothorax, pneumonia, heart failure, and escalation concern.
safetyChecks.rationale: Chest pain: Respiratory rate and visible breathing effort are early bedside markers of respiratory failure, metabolic stress, sepsis physiology, or endocrine crisis severity.
safetyChecks.management_change: Document tachypnea or respiratory distress because this changes PE, pneumothorax, pneumonia, heart failure, and escalation concern.
safetyChecks.limitations: Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
safetyChecks.diagnostic_target: tachypnea, hypoventilation, respiratory compensation, or acute illness severity

safetyChecks.label: Measure oxygen saturation
safetyChecks.action: Document hypoxemia because it changes cardiopulmonary emergency triage and PE or pneumonia concern.
safetyChecks.rationale: Chest pain: Oxygenation separates stable outpatient-style assessment from possible respiratory failure, pulmonary source, shock physiology, or need for urgent support.
safetyChecks.management_change: Document hypoxemia because it changes cardiopulmonary emergency triage and PE or pneumonia concern.
safetyChecks.limitations: Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
safetyChecks.diagnostic_target: hypoxemia, oxygen requirement, respiratory support need, or cardiopulmonary acuity

safetyChecks.label: Observe general appearance
safetyChecks.action: Document diaphoresis, distress, pallor, or toxic appearance because these change urgency and pretest concern for dangerous causes.
safetyChecks.rationale: Chest pain: Global acuity and perfusion findings identify patients who need urgent escalation before a routine focused workup is completed.
safetyChecks.management_change: Document diaphoresis, distress, pallor, or toxic appearance because these change urgency and pretest concern for dangerous causes.
safetyChecks.limitations: Global appearance and perfusion are subjective and affected by baseline function, pain, medications, temperature, and examiner experience; pair with objective vitals and reassessment.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
safetyChecks.diagnostic_target: global acuity, shock/toxic appearance, distress, or immediate safety concern

safetyChecks.label: Measure blood pressure in both arms
safetyChecks.action: Add this when aortic syndrome concern is present because a major arm-to-arm difference changes urgency and imaging pathway.
safetyChecks.rationale: Chest pain: Blood pressure anchors hemodynamic safety and helps identify shock, hypertensive urgency, orthostasis, or medication-risk context before focused diagnosis-specific decisions.
safetyChecks.management_change: Add this when aortic syndrome concern is present because a major arm-to-arm difference changes urgency and imaging pathway.
safetyChecks.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.
safetyChecks.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

synthetic_patient_scenarios.expected_active_branch: STEMI or ischemic ECG, evolving serial ECG, troponin above 99th percentile with dynamic change, HEART 7-10, hypotension/shock, recurrent syncope, or new neurologic deficit
Abrupt tearing pain/back radiation, pulse/BP differential, widened mediastinum, pleuritic hypoxemia, unilateral absent breath sounds, fever/rub, emesis, or subcutaneous emphysema
30-day death/MACE <1%, HEART <3 or EDACS <16, nonischemic ECG, serial troponins below 99th percentile, no aortic/PE features, stable symptoms, and follow-up access
HEART 4-6, nondiagnostic ECG with ongoing suspicion, unresolved troponin risk, known CAD/risk factors, or need for observation/imaging
Pregnancy/postpartum, eGFR <30, contrast allergy, BP >=200/110, SBP <90, AMI <2 days, bronchospasm, arrhythmia, caffeine within 12 hours, or local troponin protocol
Worse pain, new ischemic ECG, troponin >99th percentile or concerning delta, worse vitals/SpO2, syncope/neuro deficit, or new aortic/PE/pneumothorax signs
Symptoms stable/resolved, ECGs nonischemic, serial troponins below 99th percentile without concerning delta, HEART/EDACS low risk, and emergencies excluded
Localized tenderness, reflux/biliary pattern, dermatomal rash, pneumonia findings, or anxiety/panic pattern fits after ACS/aortic/PE/pneumothorax/esophageal/myopericarditis checks
Emergency causes excluded or managed, pending results have owners, follow-up is reachable, and return precautions are documented

clinical_cutoff_criteria.label: Acute chest pain ACS screen: 12-lead ECG reviewed within 10 minutes; cardiac troponin measured as soon as possible
clinical_cutoff_criteria.criteria_text: For acute chest pain, obtain and interpret an ECG for STEMI within 10 minutes of arrival and measure cardiac troponin as soon as possible when ACS is suspected.
data_needed: cardiac troponin assay and collection time

clinical_cutoff_criteria.label: Serial troponin rule-out/rule-in timing: hs-cTn repeat at 1-3 hours; conventional cTn repeat at 3-6 hours
clinical_cutoff_criteria.criteria_text: When serial troponins are needed to exclude myocardial injury, repeat high-sensitivity troponin at 1 to 3 hours after time zero or conventional troponin at 3 to 6 hours.

clinical_cutoff_criteria.label: Low-risk chest pain: 30-day death/MACE risk <1%, HEART <3 or EDACS <16 with serial troponins below 99th percentile
clinical_cutoff_criteria.criteria_text: Low-risk acute chest pain requires estimated 30-day death or MACE risk <1%; examples include HEART score <3 or EDACS <16 with initial and serial cTn/hs-cTn below the assay 99th percentile and no ischemic ECG change.

clinical_cutoff_criteria.label: Aortic dissection concern: abrupt severe/ripping pain plus pulse differential plus widened mediastinum gives >80% probability
clinical_cutoff_criteria.criteria_text: Abrupt severe ripping chest/back pain, pulse differential, and widened mediastinum on chest radiograph should route to acute aortic syndrome imaging; the guideline slide set reports >80% probability when severe abrupt pain, pulse differential, and widened mediastinum coexist.
clinical_cutoff_criteria.source_section: Physical examination and acute aortic syndrome
